title: Canning Foods
content:
STANDARD SIRUPS

Thin: 1 part sugar, 3 parts water
Medium: 1 part sugar, 2 parts water
Thick: 1 part sugar, 1 part water
Combine; boil 5 minutes.

FRUIT CANNING

Select well-ripened, firm fruit. Wash; pare if necessary; remove stems and blossom ends. Leave berries whole; cut other fruits to desired shape. Fill jars to within ½ inch of top with boiling sirup of desired density. Adjust rings and caps. Process according to time table for fruit type; complete seal.

VEGETABLE CANNING

Select firm, crisp vegetables; can within one hour of gathering. Wash thoroughly; scrape or pare; slice or leave whole. Cook all vegetables except tomatoes and beets 3-5 minutes in water to cover (shrinks and allows more solid pack). Beets must be cooked until skins removable. Pack cooked vegetables immediately into sterilized jars. Add 1 teaspoon salt per quart. Fill to within ½ inch of top with boiling water. Wipe jar top; adjust ring and cap; partially seal. Process according to time table; complete seal based on container type.

MEAT, POULTRY, GAME CANNING

All meats must bleed well and be thoroughly chilled before canning. Wash; do not soak in salt water. May be seared or roasted before packing, or packed raw. Pack loosely for thorough heat penetration. Fill jars to within ½ inch of top. Wipe top; add 1 teaspoon salt per pint; adjust ring and cap; partially seal. Process according to time table; complete seal based on container type.

LABELING & STORING

Uniform labels 2 inches from bottom. Name of product and date plainly lettered. Store in cool, dark place.
